one_time_description: Transfer to deferred revenue for ISC Capital projects funding received but not yet recognized
economic_notes: Band has three government business enterprises: Kumcheen Enterprises Ltd., Lytton Development Corp., and Tlkemcheen Economic Development LLP. BC First Nations Gaming revenue of $741,174 received in 2020.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020. Prior period restatement in 2019 due to change in accounting policy for Replacement Reserve Fund from equity to liability treatment per CMHC Operating Agreement. Social Development and Health are reported as a combined segment; Health expenses are included in the $3,722,067 Social Development/Health total. Capital Fund expenses of $952,578 represent amortization only. Three GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis. Annual debt service calculated as sum of principal repayments: $36,194 + $141,506 = $177,700 (capital asset loan and Social Housing Fund debt). Total government transfers estimated as ISC $7,236,936 + FNHA $1,326,984 + CMHC $148,210 + Province of BC $211,732 + Dept of Justice $85,580 + Community Partners Legal Aid $20,411 + UBCM $2,500 + FNESC $90,676 + FNLM $64,862 = $9,187,891; however BC Hydro $70,259 and Works program $50,345 may also be government transfers bringing total closer to $9,308,495. Used ISC+FNHA+CMHC+BC+DOJ+Legal Aid+UBCM+FNESC+FNLM = 9,187,891 as conservative total government transfers estimate; OSR = 11,672,679 - 9,547,897 using broader estimate including BC Hydro and Works.
